Tests the behavior of the `arguments` object in a function.

# Purpose
This code defines a function `f_arg` that takes two parameters, `x` and `y`, and returns the `arguments` object, which contains all the arguments passed to the function. The code then uses an `assert` statement to verify that when `f_arg` is called with three arguments (1, 2, 3), the third argument can be accessed through the `arguments` object, confirming that `arguments[2]` equals 3. This demonstrates the use of the `arguments` object to access function arguments beyond the explicitly defined parameters. The code provides narrow functionality, focusing on illustrating the behavior of the `arguments` object in JavaScript functions.
